Common_Cold.description: A viral infection of the upper respiratory tract affecting the nose and throat. Usually harmless and resolves within 7-10 days.
Common_Cold.suggested_action: Rest, stay hydrated, use OTC cold remedies. See a doctor if symptoms persist beyond 10 days.

Influenza.description: A contagious respiratory illness caused by influenza viruses. More severe than the common cold with sudden onset.
Influenza.suggested_action: Rest, fluids, and antiviral medications if caught early. Seek medical care for high-risk individuals.

COVID-19.description: Respiratory illness caused by the SARS-CoV-2 virus. Symptoms range from mild to severe and may include loss of taste/smell.
COVID-19.suggested_action: Isolate immediately, get tested, monitor oxygen levels. Seek emergency care for breathing difficulties.

Pneumonia.description: An infection that inflames air sacs in one or both lungs, which may fill with fluid. Can be life-threatening.
Pneumonia.suggested_action: Seek medical attention promptly. Antibiotics for bacterial pneumonia. Hospitalization may be required.

Bronchitis.description: Inflammation of the bronchial tubes that carry air to the lungs, often producing persistent cough with mucus.
Bronchitis.suggested_action: Rest, fluids, humidifier use. See a doctor if cough lasts more than 3 weeks or produces blood.

Asthma.description: A chronic condition where airways narrow, swell, and produce extra mucus, making breathing difficult.
Asthma.suggested_action: Use prescribed inhalers. Develop an asthma action plan with your doctor. Avoid known triggers.

Allergic_Rhinitis.description: Inflammation of the nasal passages caused by an allergic response to airborne substances like pollen or dust.
Allergic_Rhinitis.suggested_action: Avoid allergens, use antihistamines or nasal corticosteroids. Consider allergy testing.

Sinusitis.description: Inflammation or swelling of the tissue lining the sinuses, often following a cold, causing facial pain and congestion.
Sinusitis.suggested_action: Use saline nasal spray, decongestants, and warm compresses. See a doctor if symptoms last >10 days.

Tuberculosis.description: A serious bacterial infection primarily affecting the lungs, caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis. Highly contagious.
Tuberculosis.suggested_action: Seek immediate medical evaluation. TB requires a 6-9 month antibiotic regimen. Notify close contacts.

Malaria.description: A life-threatening mosquito-borne disease caused by Plasmodium parasites. Characterized by cyclical fever and chills.
Malaria.suggested_action: Seek urgent medical treatment. Antimalarial drugs are essential. Prevention includes mosquito nets and repellents.

Dengue_Fever.description: A mosquito-borne viral infection causing high fever, severe joint/muscle pain, and potentially hemorrhagic complications.
Dengue_Fever.suggested_action: Seek medical care immediately. Stay hydrated, use acetaminophen for pain. Avoid aspirin and NSAIDs.

Typhoid_Fever.description: A bacterial infection caused by Salmonella typhi, spread through contaminated food and water.
Typhoid_Fever.suggested_action: Seek medical diagnosis and antibiotic treatment. Maintain strict hygiene. Vaccination is recommended for travelers.

Gastroenteritis.description: Inflammation of the stomach and intestines, typically from viral or bacterial infection, causing vomiting and diarrhea.
Gastroenteritis.suggested_action: Stay hydrated with oral rehydration solutions. Eat bland foods. See a doctor if symptoms are severe or prolonged.

Urinary_Tract_Infection.description: An infection in any part of the urinary system — kidneys, ureters, bladder, or urethra. Most common in the bladder.
Urinary_Tract_Infection.suggested_action: See a doctor for antibiotics. Drink plenty of water. Seek urgent care if fever develops or pain worsens.

Migraine.description: A neurological condition characterized by intense, debilitating headaches often accompanied by nausea and light sensitivity.
Migraine.suggested_action: Rest in a dark, quiet room. Take prescribed migraine medication. Track triggers to prevent future episodes.

Hypertension.description: Persistently elevated blood pressure that increases risk of heart disease, stroke, and kidney problems. Often asymptomatic.
Hypertension.suggested_action: Monitor blood pressure regularly. Adopt a low-sodium diet, exercise, and take prescribed medications.

Diabetes_Type_2.description: A chronic metabolic condition where the body becomes resistant to insulin or doesn't produce enough, causing high blood sugar.
Diabetes_Type_2.suggested_action: Consult an endocrinologist. Monitor blood sugar, maintain a healthy diet, exercise regularly, and take prescribed medications.

Anemia.description: A condition where the blood lacks enough healthy red blood cells to carry adequate oxygen to tissues, causing fatigue.
Anemia.suggested_action: Get blood tests to determine cause. Iron supplements, dietary changes, or treating underlying conditions may be needed.
